rulings.Case Title: CHARLENE HASTINGS VS. IRENE PARSONS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rosa Parsons, Douglas Parsons Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of All Causes Of Action In Favor Of Defendants Douglas H. Parsons And Rosa Parsons Pursuant To Ccp 437(C)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2017 line 9. Defendants Rosa Parsons and Douglas Parsons' Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of All Causes Of Action In Favor Of Defendants Douglas H. Parsons And Rosa Parsons Pursuant To CCP 437(C) DENIED. Triable issues of material facts regarding existence of a landlord-tenant relationship based on ratification of the lease entered into by Irene and P; and by failing to address the discrimination against Plaintiff by Irene. Plaintiff to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437(c). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VLAD CHERNOGUS VS. SILVIA URRUTIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To The Terms Of Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment Or Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2017 line 4. PLAINTIFF VLAD CHERNOGUS' Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To The Terms Of Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment Or Dismissal DENIED. The Settlement agreement does not provide any time limitation and any enforcement mechanism other than for 180 days upon ex-parte application. The 180 days have lapsed. The Court is not aware of and Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a judgment for possession may be obtained (for a breach of an agreement to pay rent on time) by a motion to enforce at any point in time after entry into stipulation and in perpetuity, i.e. be it 9 months or 9 years. This ruling does not limit Plaintiff's rights to seek relief by other means for asserted breach of lease or settlement agreement. = (501/REQ)
